{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the information provided, fine needle aspiration cytology (FNAC) is generally effective for diagnosing various thyroid conditions, including papillary and follicular carcinomas of the thyroid. However, for breast cancer and lung adenocarcinoma, FNAC may not always provide a definitive diagnosis due to the limitations in sampling a sufficient amount of tissue. The reference to the disadvantages of FNAC in diagnosing breast masses (Document [28]) and the fact that FNAC is not sufficient for all diagnoses (Document [9]) support this conclusion.", "answer_choice": "C"}
